title: grants — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by `GrantServiceProvider::boot()` so any FormRequest / Data class can validate grant payloads. Every rule is defence-in-depth for the six-invariant guard set enforced at the observer layer — the rules run first (returning 422 with structured errors) so callers get clean feedback before the observer fires.

rules.name: valid_permission_list
rules.description: Every string in the JSONB permissions array must resolve to a live row in the `permissions` table (owned by access/rbac). Optional `resource_type` argument constrains the acceptable prefix (e.g. `valid_permission_list:invoice` requires every permission name to start with `invoice.`). Cross-referenced via PermissionCatalogueValidator.

params.name: resource_type
params.description: Optional. When supplied, enforces permission_prefix from the resource_type's Grantable attribute.

rules.default_message: One or more permissions are unknown or do not apply to :resource_type.

rules.name: grant_expires_in_future
rules.description: expires_at, when present, must be > now(). Nullable is allowed here — the rule only fires when a value is passed. The permanent_grants entitlement check happens separately (a rule cannot enforce entitlements).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be in the future.

rules.name: subject_in_same_tenant_as_resource
rules.description: Cross-field rule. Verifies (subject.tenant_id) === (resource.tenant_id) === (auth_user.tenant_id). Refuses cross-tenant grant attempts with a translated 422. The observer ALSO refuses at model layer with GRANT_CROSS_TENANT event dispatch — this rule is the friendly first-line filter.

params.name: other_field_map
params.description: Field name convention: `subject_id,resource_type,resource_id` — the resolver looks up the subject + resource by ID + type to compare tenant_ids.

rules.default_message: The subject and resource must belong to the same tenant.

rules.name: grantor_holds_permissions_being_granted
rules.description: Privilege-escalation guard. Verifies the auth_user (who will be recorded as `granted_by`) holds every permission being granted, either through RBAC roles OR through their own active allow grants. Refused otherwise with GRANT_PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION. Bypassed only when the caller has `grants.grant_across_role` + a matching approval-flow context (system-driven grants from workflow/approvals also bypass — the approval IS the justification).

params.name: permissions_field
params.description: The form field carrying the permissions array.

rules.default_message: You cannot grant permissions you do not hold.

rules.name: valid_grant_reason
rules.description: reason must be at least 20 characters (configurable via settings.grants.min_reason_length), trimmed of whitespace, not a sentinel like 'test' / 'temp' / 'because'. Enforces the audit-trail promise: every grant has a human-readable justification worth reading. The minimum is a nudge — 20 chars forces a real sentence.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be at least :min characters and describe why the grant is being issued (avoid placeholder text).

rules.name: grantable_resource_registered
rules.description: Cross-field rule. Verifies (a) resource_type is registered in GrantableRegistry, (b) resource_id resolves to a live row of that type in the caller's tenant. Refused with GRANT_RESOURCE_TYPE_UNREGISTERED (unknown type) or RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND (unknown id).

params.name: resource_type_field
params.description: Field name carrying the resource_type value.

params.name: resource_id_field
params.description: Field name carrying the resource_id value.

rules.default_message: The specified resource is not grantable or does not exist.

rules.name: grantable_subject_type
rules.description: Verifies subject_type is in the registered subject-type list. Wave 1b: only `user`. Future: `group`. Refused with GRANT_SUBJECT_TYPE_UNSUPPORTED for anything else.
rules.default_message: The subject type ':value' is not supported.

usage_from_data_class: The CreateGrantData / UpdateGrantData / BulkIssueGrantsData spatie-data classes register these rules via their `rules()` method. This file is the canonical registry for the underlying rule implementations.
